name: deterministic-bootstrap-seeding-via-sha256
description: Cross-process-reproducible RNG seeding for archive-anchored comparators: derive a 64-bit seed by SHA-256-ing the comparison-identifying tuple (e.g. a_candidate_id || b_candidate_id || split_id) and taking the first 8 bytes. Python's builtin hash() is salted per interpreter via PYTHONHASHSEED, so it cannot anchor a stable seed across processes — using it would silently invalidate any baseline registry that compares CI bounds across runs. Used in packages/vigor-harness/src/vigor_harness/comparator.py:_seeded_rng for the paired-bootstrap CI on Δmean_composite.

name: getattr-forward-compat-for-strict-pydantic-schemas
description: Forward-compat hook for ADR-0011 strict Pydantic schemas (extra=forbid): consumers that need to optionally observe a field that does not yet exist on the v1 model use getattr(model, 'field_name', None). Because the v1 model has extra='forbid', a v1 payload cannot smuggle the field through model_extra — but a future v2 model that declares the field will return it via the normal attribute lookup, so the consumer code does not need to change when the v2 schema lands. Used in comparator.py:_coerce_manifest_sha to pre-stage the manifest_sha256 equality check ADR-0031 §2 step 1 calls for once HarnessEvalReport.v2 lands.
